rules: Stages are sequential; no skipping a stage that produced state.
Each stage has explicit supervision posture and evidence requirements.
Promotion to the next stage requires a documented exit decision with provenance.
Demotion (to a prior stage) is always allowed and is the safe response to incidents.
Controlled production remains under continuous governance; it is never a terminal stage of governance.

evidence: no blocking risks for the slice
stages.supervision: unsupervised allowed only for read-only slices
supervised by an operator at every boundary
supervised end-to-end; explicit-action gate required for any destructive extension
supervised; bounded user population; explicit success criteria

stages.exit_to: (no further stage; remains under continuous governance)
stages.supervision: supervised by SRE/operator on-call; defined kill-switch
